entry.title: SF mobile app download and usage monitoring
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Is there a way to report on SF mobile app download and usage?

I know that in manage mobile user, we can export all mobile users, however, is there a way to know who is actively using the mobile application (usage)?

Many thanks!

Best Regards,

Gelo

entry.title: Re: New Webinar Series: Architecting Your SAP Business Data Cloud Journey – From Data to Intelligenc
entry.content: The registration confirmation message that comes back from registering has a calendar file with all the events. Each calendar entry has a link to the webinar broadcast.

entry.title: Re: Time sheet and overtime automatic submission and appr...
entry.content:
Issue:

  • Salary must be calculated on the 1st of each month.
  • Overtime (OT) for the end-of-month (e.g., 30th/31st) is often not approved in time for payroll.
  • Timesheets are used (basic EC Time Sheet, not Time Tracking), and rely on Monday manager approval.
  • This causes delays in including OT for the final 1-2 days of the month in payroll.

Ensure OT (especially for month-end dates) is approved and included automatically or faster, so payroll on the 1st includes all relevant earnings.

 Auto-Approval with Manager Notification

You can remove the workflow for overtime approvals and replace it with an automated approval, while still notifying the manager for visibility.

  1. Set Up Manager Notification (Optional)
    • Use a Business Rule on onSave or onChange of the Timesheet.
    • The rule can trigger a notification to the manager (without requiring their approval).
    • Use "Send Email Notification" in the rule and target the Manager field.
    • This ensures 30th and 31st OT gets picked up by payroll on the 1st.

 Use a one-step auto-escalation workflow:

  • Set manager approval to be auto-approved after 1 day.

entry.title: Leave of Absence management
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have a customer that does most of their reporting based on the Position object, and they do not wish to use the standard Time Type Leave of Absence feature to maintain employees on LOAs.

Rather, they would like to use a workaround to move the employee to a position titled "Leave of Absence" so as to report as to who and how many employees are on LOA, and that the customer would like to understand if this would lead to any errors and if this solution is better in comparison to using the standard LOA time type.

SAP have indicated this is not best practice but the customer does not want to enable Time Off Time Types and change how they manage LOAs in the system currently.

Does anyone have any experience using a workaround for LOAs and whether this would be a suitable solution for the customer's needs?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Tips to assess and plan global e-invoicing solution implementation based on SAP Document and Rep
entry.content:
Nice article. And thanks a lot for sharing your professional experience. The DRC knowledge banks is still niche, and. you have clearly elaborated all possible scenarios and technical touch points. 

Wish you keep contributing as great as this article and many more like that for the community benefit. 

All my best wishes. 

Kind regards, 

Satish

entry.title: Sourcing and Procuremant
entry.content: When making procurement such as placing an order from a quotation within SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, is there a report function that allows you to check the difference between the quotation amount and the order amount when ordering a certain item, as well as the discount rate?

entry.title: Re: CoA is not printing automatically after Post Goods is...
entry.content: Make sure you have a valid output condition record in place and check that output requirement 001 is assigned to the output in the message schema.

entry.title: Group Reporting - Formula wizard in Review Booklet
entry.content:
Hi,

I was looking for some guidance on the formula wizard that is available in the Review Booklet when we try to add a calculation. 

We are looking for adding a calculated FS item for eg: Cost/ Revenue

Any suggestions or documentation reference will be of great help. 

Thanks,

Massarrat

entry.title: BTP App assignment in S4HC Custom Business Roles
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have few apps in BTP UI5 apps those need to be integrated with S4HC custom business roles. Can you provide help with process to archive this requirement.

Could you please provide assistance? Your prompt help would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks & Regards,

Veera Anasuri


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BTP App assignment in S4HC Custom Business Roles
entry.content:
If your apps are deployed on BTP, please kindly try to use "Custom Tiles" to create custom tiles.

Custom tiles can be used to access external applications.

And then you can assign the new tile to the business catalog where the new tile should be integrated by clicking Assign Catalogs in the custom tile details, or by launching the Custom Catalog Extensions app in the SAP Fiori Launchpad.

You can find more information about this in the Help Portal Page below:

Custom Tiles | SAP Help Portal

Custom Catalog Extensions | SAP Help Portal

Thanks and Best Regards,

Bell

entry.title: Re: Integrate SAP CRM WEB UI in BTP Build Work Zone
entry.content: Any update on this? Solved?

entry.title: Re: Certificate C_S4CPB_2502
entry.content: thank you RaghavaReddy. I have used the workaround, given by you, and I was able to perform the Task. Cheers, Dorota

entry.title: Re: DATA RELEASE FROM ACDOCA OF MULTIPLE CLIENTS TO ACDOCU.
entry.content:
Hi,

this is not possible. The 'Release UJ' task can only transfer the data from ACDOCA to ACDOCU when Accounting (ACDOCA) is in the same client as Group Reporting (ACDOCU).

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Configure outbound Scenario from S/4 to CPI
entry.content:
Hi Shashak,

Could you provide some additional context on what you are trying to achieve, so that we could provide a proper response to your question.

Thanks,
Prasanth

entry.title: Supporting the Cloud-Journey: A New Chapter for our SAP HANA Certification
entry.content:
As part of our SAP Certification for Operations Partners program, we offer a range of certifications that recognize partners delivering high-quality services for SAP® solutions and supporting customers on their digital transformation journeys. To reflect the growing importance of cloud technologies, we’ve updated our SAP HANA operations certification, now titled “SAP HANA Cloud and database operations.”

The cloud is no longer a destination but a continuous journey of optimization and innovation. To help our partners confidently lead their customers on this path, we’ve enhanced the certification to focus on the practical, day-to-day realities of SAP HANA Cloud deployments.

The updated certification places stronger emphasis on cloud-related content and highlights the core competencies required for:

  • Seamless migrations to SAP HANA Cloud
  • Operational management of SAP HANA Cloud instances, including performance monitoring and security

By demonstrating these skills, partners reinforce their role in confidently guiding customers through every phase of their cloud journey.

With its expanded scope, the certification now reflects a partner’s end-to-end expertise across the SAP HANA Cloud lifecycle - serving as a strong differentiator in delivering sustained value in the cloud.

Existing certified partners will benefit from these enhancements at no additional cost and will complete the extended content during their next re-certification audit.

 

SAP Certifications for Operations Partners - overview of certifications

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Information on the certification program for operations partners and an overview of all certified partners can be found in the SAP Operations Partner Guide.

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP mock REST service

entry.title: sap hana sd pending sales orders where dispatch not done order wise material wise qty and value rep
entry.content:
SAP HANA /SD 

 

pending order status 

using v_ra  backorders which does not have value

vl10c --> Sales order to delivery pending 

vl09 delivery order pending status 

appears not possible in Standard 

Acutal need is Sales order orderd qty, value Dev=livery Qty , value, Despatch qty value, pending order qty value material wise 

is there any possibility to get a query for this ?

entry.title: Unplanned depreciation calculation method
entry.content:
Dear Export,

I have an asset where usually do planned depreciation by every month. i have assigned Depreciation key as is day by day key. Unfortunately, business requirement is to post extra depreciation for that asset. 

So, i have posted Unplanned Depreciation by Transaction type 640 and when we refresh AW01N T.code against Asset planned depreciation. It got changed and next month planned value has been reduced.

How it is happened and is there any calculation method for next month planned depreciation.

entry.title: Can't see invoices in SAP for Me even if I have all authorizations
entry.content:
Can't see invoices under Finance & Legal -> Billing tab.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Can't see invoices in SAP for Me even if I have all a...
entry.content:
If you have questions or issues regarding the actual billing/invoices in the Billing application of SAP for Me (e.g. General invoice questions, missing invoices, too many invoices, invoice inaccuracies, questions on paid invoices, etc.), please email the SAP Cash Collection team directly at FinanceAR@sap.com (they do not work support cases).

In the email, provide your invoice/billing questions, your customer number/name and full details and screenshots of the invoice/invoice number.

Alternatively, you can also email the country-specific cash collection team found in the attachment added to this article.

3107749 - How to reach the SAP Cash Collection Team for billing questions - SAP for Me - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: Service call module update date and create time into...
entry.content:
Hi

try this

SELECT
  OSCL.UpdateDate,
  OSCL.UpdateTime,

  RIGHT('0000' + CAST(OSCL.UpdateTime AS VARCHAR), 4) AS ZeitString,
  
  
  CAST(
    CONVERT(VARCHAR(10), OSCL.UpdateDate, 120) + ' ' +
    LEFT(RIGHT('0000' + CAST(OSCL.UpdateTime AS VARCHAR), 4), 2) + ':' +
    RIGHT(RIGHT('0000' + CAST(OSCL.UpdateTime AS VARCHAR), 4), 2) + ':00'
    AS DATETIME
  ) AS DatumTime

FROM OSCL
WHERE CallID = 1

regards Lothar

entry.title: SF Onboarding → Celonis via API – only few tasks visible
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We're currently connecting SuccessFactors Onboarding to Celonis via API, but only get:

  • 2 tasks from “Data Collection”
  • 1 out of 6 “New Hire Tasks”

These show up in Celonis, but only partially – e.g., titles, comments, and content are missing. Other tasks (visible in SF under “Manage Data” with Process ID) don’t come through the API at all.

Question:
Has anyone managed to pull more tasks or fields? Any workarounds (OData, Integration Center, Reports etc.)?

Thanks for sharing your experience!

Best Regards,

Svetlana

SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding API OData

entry.title: Re: New Pipeline for Cloud Integration features with version 1.0.11
entry.content: I am approaching the pipeline and therefore I am not a super expert. I have a doubt: when I access for Partner Altervative, the values ​​to use for recipient URL or for other parameters are those contained in the "string" tab ?. Do the groovy provided by the templates in Business SAP HUB reason in this way? In general, there is a guideline of which values ​​to insert in each field?. Example: Agency: sender system, Schema: Sender interface, ID: Namespace, etc..

entry.title: Lack of API Support for Invoice Cancellation in SAP Convergent Invoicing
entry.content:
We are currently attempting to cancel invoices in Convergent Invoicing directly from the front-end system. However, we've discovered that SAP does not provide any standard APIs to support this functionality. This is quite surprising, considering that invoice cancellation is a fundamental requirement in most billing scenarios.

Due to the absence of standard APIs, we are now exploring the development of a custom API. However, we’ve encountered another challenge: the standard function modules (FMs) required for invoice cancellation are not released for customer use. As a result, there's effectively no supported way to perform invoice cancellations outside of using transaction codes or Fiori applications.

This raises a significant concern. If we proceed with a custom solution using non-released FMs, we risk losing SAP support in the event of issues—even if those issues originate from standard SAP code. The typical response from SAP in such cases is that the FM is not released and, therefore, not supported.

Given that no alternative standard option is provided for such a basic requirement, we are looking for guidance on how this scenario can be handled while still remaining within SAP's support boundaries. Is there a recommended or supported approach to achieve invoice cancellation programmatically, or any way to escalate this gap for consideration?

entry.title: Re: Final settlement is not considering the previous sett...
entry.content: I have checked all the configuration mentioned above nothing is wrong. In one condition contract, i could see RES2 getting determined in one settlement document and not in another settlement document. Also, RES2 got determined only in the case where the Sold to mentioned in the Business volume criteria is same as Bill to party in Settlement Document. Kindly provide your inputs.

entry.title: Saudi Arabia 2024 Labor Law Updates: What’s New for Time Management in EC
entry.content:
Focus of this blog

In response to the 2024 updates to Saudi Arabia’s Labor Law, SAP continues its commitment to delivering compliant and streamlined HR solutions tailored to local legal requirements. As part of this effort, we have implemented key configuration updates in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central (EC) to reflect the latest legal mandates.

1. Probation period

Business Needs:

  • Based on recent labor law changes, the probation period for regular employees will now be 180 days from the hire date.
  • The probation period will be extended if any holidays or sick leave occur during this time.
1.1. Now let's implement the first part “default 180 days”

First, use the date field in Job Information to capture the end of the probation period based on the employee’s hire date.

 

 

| Notes:

  • Check the field using Manage Business Configuration > Job Information > Take Action.
  • Don’t forget to assign this field to the appropriate role via Manage Permission Roles.

Next, reuse the business rule “DefultProbationEndDateHire” and modify it to default the date to 180 days from hiring

Default Probation End Date Hire Business rule snapshot

 

| Note: this rule can be found under latest Saudi Arabia Best Practice

And this what you will get

 

1.2. So how to extend the probation period?

As per Saudi labor law, if an employee takes sick leave during the probation period, or if any part of their probation period overlaps with official public holidays, the probation period will be impacted. In such cases, the duration of the probation must be extended accordingly.

To accommodate this, we need to implement several configurations in the system to automatically extend the probation period in the following scenarios:

  1. If the employee requests or takes sick leave during the probation period.
  2. If any part of the probation period falls during an official public holiday.

1.2.1. Leave of Absence “Sick” for extending Probation period

For better solution we advise to create new event reason for sick leave in probation period to be recorded in job info once employee raise a request

Assign the appropriate event reason in the Leave of Absence Event Reason (Plan) field, which can be found under the Sick Leave time type configuration settings.

| Note:

  • it is recommended to create a specific sick leave type for employees in their probation period. This will help:
  • Easily identify and monitor sick leave taken during probation.
  • Ensure the correct adjustment of the probation period.

 

| Note: before we start creating this business rule make sure that you configured the holiday calendar

1.2.2. Extend Probation Period: sick leave and Holiday Calendar business rule:

Scenario Type: Trigger onSave for HRIS Elements
Base Object: Job Information Model
Rule Trigger: When a Job Information record is saved or updated

Case 1: No Leave of Absence

  • Company = your legal entity
  • Holiday Calendar = SAU_HOLIDAY_CAL
  • No leave of absence return date

Set Probationary Period End Date = Hiring Date + 180 + Holidays

Case 2: Employee Returned from Leave

  • Company = your legal entity
  • Holiday Calendar = SAU_HOLIDAY_CAL
  • Leave of absence return date is present

Set Probationary Period End Date = Hiring Date + 180 + Holidays + Sick Leave Days

 

Let’s test, just hire new employee

 

| Note: This rule should be triggered OnSave under Job Information in Business Configuration tool so it will calculate the probation period end date in case you edit and save the data

One more test, create a Sick leave request

 

Now check the Job Information update history

| Note: You have to set the actual return date after saving request , so the event reason (return from leave) will be activated.

 

 2. Compassionate Leave Take Rule

Business Needs: Compassionate Leave Based on Relationship

As per the new Saudi labor law, Compassionate Leave entitlements must be granted based on the employee's relationship to the relative.

  1. 5 Days of Compassionate Leave are granted if the relative is: Spouse, Father, Mother, Son or Daughter.
  2. 3 Days of Compassionate Leave are granted if the relative is: Brother or Sister.

2.1. Required fields for validation

Create a specific field for selecting the relationship type if doesn’t exist.

| Note: Don’t forget to update the relationship picklist

2.2. Compassionate Leave Configuration Based on Relationship business rule :

Scenario: Absence Validation

Add if condition for the time type “Companionate Leave (SA-COMPA)”.

 

 

Here is the result:

 

3. Maternity Leave Validation

Business Needs:

According to the new Saudi labor law, a female employee is entitled to 12 weeks (84 days) of maternity leave, to be taken under specific conditions:

  1. Pre-Delivery Period: The employee can request maternity leave before the delivery date. Minimum: 28 days / Maximum: 42 days.
  2. Post-Delivery Period: The remaining portion of maternity leave must be taken after the delivery date. Minimum: 42 days / Maximum: 56 days.

3.1. Configure Expected Delivery Date

Create a specific field for selecting the Expected Delivery date

 

3.2 Maternity Leave Business Rule

Scenario: Absence Validation

Time Type: Maternity Leave (SA-MATER)

Validation:

  • Total leave duration (84 days)
  • Allowed pre-delivery period (between 28 and 42 days)

 Case 1: if all conditions are correct system will proceed with the request

 

Case 2: If Expected delivery date Less than 28 system will drop an error

Case3: if Expected delivery date more than 42 system will drop an error

Case 4: If Maternity leave more than 84 system will drop an error 

 

 

Takeaways

With the updates outlined in this blog, you are well-positioned to ensure compliance with the 2024 Saudi labor law changes in the area of Time Management. Additional implementation guidance covering other legal areas — including Violations and Sanctions — will follow shortly.

We encourage you to subscribe to SAP’s Regulatory Change Manager (RCM) to stay informed about future updates. As always, your feedback is invaluable — share your thoughts and suggestions to help us continuously shape and refine the best-fit localization solutions.

entry.title: Re: Attachment field for Large Object not visible in service metadata
entry.content: I am also facing this issue in unmanaged scenarios when I created attachment column as rawstring ...it's not visible in service metadata also in ui preview it's displayed but while creating new record only icon appearing upload file pop-up also not appearing..

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite - Message Metric Question?
entry.content:
Hello,

we have an iFlow in Integration Suite which transfers binary data from ECC to a Cloud System.
The binary date is transported in the body of the message.
SAP Note 2942344 describes the measurement of a message:
"......If an integration message is larger than 250 kilobytes, any amount in excess of 250 kilobytes will be charged as one additional message for each 250 kilobytes or portion thereof......"

If we want to transfer a file with 30MB as binary data SAP measures 120 messages (30.000 kilobytes / 250 kilobytes)! Is this correct?

Is there anywhere in iflows or the integration suite where you can see how large a transmitted message was?

Thanks

entry.title: Optimizing Contingent Worker Management in SAP SuccessFactors: Preventing Licensing and Data Pitfall
entry.content:
Introduction

Contingent workers have become a core part of today’s flexible workforce. SAP SuccessFactors offers dedicated functionality to manage contingent workers separately from full-time employees (FTEs). However, incorrect configuration can lead to license mismanagement, reporting inaccuracies, and non-compliance. In this blog, I share a real-world scenario where contingent workers were mistakenly configured as FTEs and the step-by-step process I followed to resolve it.

 

The Problem: Contingent Workers Consuming FTE Licenses

At a client site, contingent workers were mistakenly onboarded using hiring actions designed for FTEs—such as "Hire - New Hire" and "Hire - Replacement." This led to a cascade of issues:

 

  • Consumed FTE licenses unintentionally
  • Were counted incorrectly in organizational reporting
  • Showed active status in the system even after separation

The root cause was a misunderstanding of how contingent worker events differ from FTE actions in SAP SuccessFactors.

 

Business Impact

This configuration error caused ripple effects across multiple business functions:

  • Licensing Cost Overruns: Unnecessary usage of paid FTE licenses
  • Compliance Risks: Active records without valid contracts or dates
  • Data Integrity Issues: Inaccurate workforce analytics and planning reports

 

Resolution Strategy: How We Fixed It Step-by-Step

Here’s the remediation process I followed, based on SAP best practices:

          Step 1: Extract Existing Job Information

  • Navigate to Reporting > All Reports
  • Run and download the Job Info export to identify affected records

     

    Step 2: Prepare Corrected Job History Template

    • Go to Import Employee Data
    • Download the Job Information Template
    • Populate the correct event reasons (e.g., "Start CWK" and proper termination reasons)

  • Ensure all actions are in sequential order

    Step 3: Re-import the Job Information

    • Import the corrected template
    • Use Full Purge to replace faulty job Information entries
    • Validate that contingent workers now reflect correct statuses and do not consume FTE licenses
    • This fix was tested and confirmed in a sandbox before applying to production.

    In line with best practices, this is the recommended approach to resolve the existing contingent worker data and accurately update their termination status.

     

    Best Practice: Hiring a Contingent Worker the Right Way

    To prevent recurrence, we established a proper process aligned with SAP best practices:

    Step 1: Initiate from Contingent Worker Template

    • Navigate to Add Contingent Worker

           Step 2: Fill Personal & Assignment Information

  • Select event reason Start CWK

 

  • Enter Contract End Date in both Assignment Info and Work Order section

    Step 3: Submit

    • Review the details and click Submit
    • The system will now correctly identify this worker as contingent, not consuming FTE license.

 

Key Takeaways

  • Always align event reasons with worker types (FTE vs. CWK)
  • Align with HR, IT, and system integrators to maintain consistent data entry standards.
  • Schedule routine audits to catch anomalies early before they escalate

Conclusion & Call to Action

A seemingly small configuration gap can result in real business cost and risk. Thankfully, SAP SuccessFactors provides the tools needed—what’s critical is following the right methodology. I hope this experience helps your organization avoid these pitfalls and confidently manage contingent workers the right way.

Have you dealt with similar issues in your SuccessFactors setup? Share your experiences or questions in the comments—let’s learn from each other!

 

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central  SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding

Welcome to SAP Community. Nice to meet you!

You should then be able to receive email notifications for community questions or blog posts on things you may be working on.

entry.title: Additional Binding annotation not working for consumption valuehepdefinition
entry.content:
I am trying to use a search help with values being filtered on an input local field value.

 

@ui.selectionField: [{position: 20}]

@ui.lineItem: [{ position: 20, label: 'Version' }]

@ui.identification: [{ position: 20, label: 'Version' }]

@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [

{ entity:{ element: 'Version',

name: 'ZPSBC_ITEMX_SH' } },

 

{ additionalBinding: [{ localElement: 'Aadhar',

element: 'AadharID',

usage: #FILTER_AND_RESULT }] }

]

Version;

 

------

 

The Search Help CDS looks like as below-

 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED

@EndUserText.label: 'ZPSBC_ITEMX_SH'

@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true

@ObjectModel.usageType:{

serviceQuality: #X,

sizeCategory: #S,

dataClass: #MIXED

}

 

@search.searchable: true

define view entity ZPSBC_ITEMX_SH as select distinct from zpsb_item

{

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Aadhar ID'

key aadhar as AadharID,

 

@search.defaultSearchElement: true

@search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.9

@EndUserText.label: 'Version of the ID'

Key version as Version

}

 

group by

aadhar,

version

-------

I am checking the SAP documentation and I believe I have done it right but stil

the value is not filtered. I need help please. I am finding this ABAP RAP trial

totally junk, sometimes despite the right code some feature won't work then wait

next day it starts working. This is science right not some dark magic. Why should

it behave like this? Please help me with this annotation and if I am doing wrong.

Does this help?

Kind regards,

Wolfgang

entry.title: Re: Introducing new type of Data Access Control "Operator and Value" for SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
@heiko_schneider - Great Article

You wrote above 

"What should be demonstrated in the example above is that is it possible to define a set of logically combined authorisations for a given user. If there are multiple dimensions to be checked for a view where this DAC will be assigned. However it is possible to define multiple conditions on one dimension (e.g. a user is allowed to ee Product Groups between 1-10 AND/OR 20-30)"

It's not possible for me to get this to work. I have this example 1:

Restriction = 1:

Criteria column COMP_CODE, operator = EQ, First_value = 1200

Criteria column PROFIT_CTR, operator = EQ, First Value = 0081101500 

Criteria column PROFIT_CTR, operator = EQ, First value = 0081301000 

This combination will deny the user to see any data at all (because a profit_ctr cannot be 2 values at the same time (AND))

Example 2 below works perfectly:

Restriction = 1:

Criteria column COMP_CODE, operator = EQ, First_value = 1200

Criteria column PROFIT_CTR, operator = EQ, First Value = 0081101500 

Restriction = 2:

Criteria column COMP_CODE, operator = EQ, First_value = 1200

Criteria column PROFIT_CTR, operator = EQ, First value = 0081301000 

So are you sure that it should be possible to define multiple conditions for the same criteria column. It makes totally sence to me, but unfortunally I cannot make it work.

Regards

Erik

entry.title: Re: Let's get to know each other!
entry.content:
Hi @DebashishMajumder ,

It's great to hear about your journey in the SAP world— sounds like an amazing career path you've had! Your experience and insights could really make a difference for others looking to navigate similar transformations. We'd love to hear your tips or success stories. Don't hesitate to share or write a blog/discussion post about it. 

Best regards,
Shreenith

entry.title: Cloud to Cloud的数据直连集成：PO/SO转换
entry.content:
这篇文章是作为Cloud to Cloud直接集成的第三篇介绍，前面两篇关于产品主数据和BP主数据的集成可参考以下链接：

Cloud to Cloud的数据直连集成：产品主数据

Cloud to Cloud的数据直连集成：业务伙伴Business Partner主数据

 

1. 背景介绍

除了之前介绍的主数据集成，另外一个常见的场景是关于贸易公司和生产公司之间采购订单（PO）与销售订单（SO）的集成：当贸易公司生成采购订单，立刻集成到生产公司生成一张销售订单（SO）。两个系统间PO/SO的集成，可以实现Cloud到Cloud直连的方式，不需要借助中间平台即可实现。

 

2. 发送系统的设置

在这个集成场景中，我们借助了两个标准scope item来实现PO到SO的转换。

在发送系统中，需要往外发送采购订单（PO），参考的scope item是2EJ（Integration of Procurement with External Suppliers）。

 

2.1 创建通信系统

如果有实现之前主数据集成的场景，那么可以复用之前的通信系统。该通信系统是需要集成的目标系统。

输入自定义的System ID、System Name，Host Name为接收系统（receiver system）的地址，维护字段Logical System和Business System。

另外，需要在通信系统中维护通信的inbound user和outbound user。

这里需要特别注意的是，发送系统的outbound user与之后维护的接收系统的inbound user需要一致。

 

2.2 创建通信安排

参考scope item 2EJ，创建基于通信场景SAP_COM_0224的通信安排。在该通信安排中，使用到的是下面这个outbound service：

 

2.3 输出参数定义（Output Parameter Determination）

进入Fiori应用“输出参数定义Output Parameter Determination”，配置采购订单EDI相关的内容。具体可参考scope item 2EJ配置文档的2.2.16章节：

2.4 给用户分配收件人（Assign Recipients）

为了后续能够看到SOAP接口的消息，打开应用Assign Recipients to Users，给测试的用户分配以下条目：

 

3. 接收系统的设置

在接收系统中，我们将会接收从发送系统发出的采购订单，在接收系统内创建销售订单。参考的scope item是2EL（Integration of Sales with External Buyers）。

3.1 创建通信系统

在接收系统中，我们首先也是创建通信系统。同样，在该通信系统中，需要维护inbound user和outbound user。 inbound user需要特别注意，与之前发送系统的outbound user维护成一致。

 

3.2 创建通信安排

参考scope item 2EL，创建基于通信场景SAP_COM_0223的通信安排。在该通信安排中，使用到的是下面这个inbound service：

3.3 给用户分配收件人（Assign Recipients）

为了后续能够看到SOAP接口的消息，打开应用Assign Recipients to Users，给测试的用户分配以下条目：

 

在完成了上述的配置之后，发送系统创建采购订单，在采购订单审批完成之后，会自动触发该接口，在接收系统创建销售订单。

 

 

4. 参考文档

如文章开头所说，实现Cloud to cloud的PO转SO的集成，参考的是标准scope item 2EJ和2EL，如想了解详细内容，可参考以下链接：

2EJ_Set-Up_EN_XX.pdf

2EL_Set-Up_EN_XX.pdf

感谢阅读！

entry.title: Re: Let's get to know each other!
entry.content:
Hi @AdityaJairath,

Thank you for joining our group and welcome to the SAP Community - it's fantastic to have you with us.
To stay updated and engage with the latest on SAP Signavio, be sure to check out the SAP Signavio topic page as well.

 Looking forward to your participation and insights!

entry.title: Re: Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps - Mobile Apps for iOS and Android
entry.content:
Hi @LudoNoens ,

Thanks for the informative post. In SAP BTP trail account before build the android app configuration I tried to enable Mobile services in SAP Build Apps and getting below error.

I have raised this question in forum as well.

error-while-enable-mobile-services-in-sap-btp-build-apps-trail-account 

Please help in this issue. Thanks in Advance.

Thanks,

Vigneshwaran

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation: Why is SAP Desktop Agen...
entry.content:
Hi Adam,

Whenever an automation is first created as an artifact in the project, you would notice that the agent version is determined at that point. This is because, the way the automation works is that it would download the package on the local system where the agent is located. These javascript files are then executed.

If you want an automation to be totally on cloud, the only way that I could think of this happening is to use action projects in conjunction with decisions to create a process flow. However, you would be limited to a lot of dependency on Decisions created, and even then, some functionalities easily achievable through custom scripts might elude you.

Do let me know if the action project + decision + forms (workflow) works for your purpose. It would be quite interesting to note how complex you could make your scenarios while still working totally on cloud based executions.

Best Regards,

Rohit

entry.title: Re: Can we able to edit standard CDS in public cloud?
entry.content: thanks, Vijay. can we able to create custom action button in standard application? any blogs you know?

entry.title: Get the error "cannot commit package xxx and earlier packages in the sequence" in replication flow
entry.content: During the initial load using a replication flow with SLT in SAP Datasphere I keep getting the error message "Cannot commit package xxx and earlier packages in the sequence/ Cannot lock packages for buffer / Lock on table DHRDB_PACKAGE failed. This only happens for the bigger tables (like VBAK, VBAP, BSEG, ACDOCA), smaller tables are working fine. Does anyone faced the same problem?

entry.title: Re: Template Allocation and Splitting Structure
entry.content:
Hi,
Thank you for the response.

We currently maintained one GL account against each Activity Type ensuring there is no overlapping of GLs in the splitting structure.  Still while running KSS2, amount posted against the GLs mapped to indirect activities are getting distributed equally.

In addition, we tried changing the formula in template allocation from Order Total Quantity to Labor Activity Quantity, but it is throwing the below error:

Kindly advise o how to resolve these issues. 

Regards

entry.title: MM and FI Interface in Goods Receipt Posting
entry.content:
Dear community, 

in the past we had SAP WM active in our Warehouses without HU Management. In this scenario we are posting our goods receipts for one Delivery Note. With this we have a 1 to 1 assignment between Goods receipt posting / material document , delivery note and invoice quantity.

We started now with an EWM roll-out in all our warehouses. Here we have now the scenario, that we are posting the Goods receipt in EWM for each Top Handling Unit. That means we got several goods receipt postings / material documents for every delivery note and invoice quantity. This sceanrio leads to an addtional effort in finance department. It is now much more complex to check goods receipt postings with the delivery note quantity and invoice quantity. Also the Goods Receipt Account Transparency is much more complex. As a last point we also have the issue, that in second scenario we cannot use our automatic Invoicing process, because here the system is checking the quantity on the delivery note / Invoice and goods receipt posting quantity.

Is this the SAP standard behaviuor? Or is there any improvement option. E.g. to merge somehow the Goods Receipt Postings in MM-FI Interface.

Thank you in advance for any hint.

Regards Elias

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Configuration in SAP Integration Suite: One Tenant, Multiple Environments
entry.content:
@EuricoBorges you can have multiple integration processes (and therefore multiple sender adapters) in one iflow. So there is no problem to address calls from different environments (dev, test, prod). 

But major flaw is the fact that every change, every deploy address all 3 systems - which is not ideal. 

But I can imagine, that there are very stable iflows which dont need change for a years, and in this case, it could be ok.

entry.title: Defect management in sap s/4 hana public cloud
entry.content:
Hi experts,

Could you please suggest, how to manage defects management in sap s/4 Hana public cloud 2502?

entry.title: Re: Actions in SAP Build Process Automation -- Need to ca...

The action project doesnt look correct to me.

1. Could you please share the full URL maintained in the destination.

2. How was the action project created? And what is the URL prefix used while creating the action project?

3. How were the actions added in the project? Manually (custom) or through already available actions?

Best Regards,

Rohit

However, I could only find the installation steps.

I'm specifically looking to understand how to push ABAP code from Eclipse (ADT) to GitHub for version control.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: mass update allowed products, replenishment rules
entry.content:
How to mass add Allowed Products or Replenishment Rules for activate logistics areas.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: mass update allowed products, replenishment rules
entry.content:
Dear customer,

 

Hope you are doing good.

We can use migration tool to mass add new location and update allowed products and add replenishment rules, but we are able to enhance already activated logistics areas through the Migration of Location and Logistics Area in the system. This is system designed.

I check the Service Explorer, no suitable API supports to mass add new allowed products and replenishment rules for already activated logistics areas. We also unable to do that via Mass Data Maintenance .

Customer have to add new allowed products and replenishment rules manually for those activated logistics areas.

Thanks for your understanding.

 

Best regards,
Aurora
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Become an Early Adopter for Joule in SAP Datasphere

Customer 

It'd be best if both documentations were fully reviewed by your team, so you can fully understand if the options provided by the Migration Object suits your business needs.

I hope this helps with your query!

Best regards,
Latha

entry.title: Re: Service call module update date and create time into...
entry.content:
Hi,

You just need to change the format from '##:##' to '00:00':

FORMAT(CONVERT(DATETIME, CONVERT(VARCHAR(10), OSCL.UpdateDate, 101)+' '+FORMAT(OSCL.UpdateTime, '00:00')) AT TIME ZONE 'Eastern Standard Time' AT TIME ZONE 'UTC', 'MM/dd/yyyy HH:mm:ss')

entry.title: Re: SAP Business ByDesign - understand the new Archive Offerings

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite - Message Metric Question?
entry.content:
@ADO 

you can go to Security - you can see Message Metric tile and pick a Iflow to see the KB of the file size transferred

Any Custom Iflows or Standard Iflow is modified - SAP note provides how it get charged

  • https://me.sap.com/notes/2942344/E
  • https://me.sap.com/notes/3461268/E

entry.title: How to check if standard Fiori App is using SOAP API
entry.content: Our client is transitioning to a new integration platform that no longer supports legacy IDOCs and SOAP APIs. We have deployed several standard Fiori applications for them. Most of the Fiori apps for sure uses oData servicse (REST API) but i guess there's a few that does not use REST. We would like to find out if there is a way to identify whether any of these Fiori apps rely on SOAP APIs. This will help us determine which APIs need to be migrated to REST.

entry.title: Re: 500 Internal Server Error Failed to process request
entry.content: Had a similar problem, the issue was space. 100% /usr/sap

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for April – SAP Build 👷‍♀️🏠
entry.content: @Dan_Wroblewski - Thanks for creating such a great learning experience. #Escaped SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Re: Allow rounding at the header level for cash payments only
entry.content: Hi, Is this still the same situation? We are in Australia and have not had 1c pieces for decades now, we need to round cash payments to the nearest 5c and this should be a standard capability, not via a plugin.

entry.title: Re: New Pipeline for Cloud Integration features with version 1.0.11
entry.content:
@dorino_digiacomo if you are new to the pipeline concept, best is to start with this blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/introducing-the-new-pipeline-concept-in-cloud-integration/ba-p/13639651, there you find also links pointing to the standard documentation and to my github repository where you find a couple of sample scenarios to understand how to setup a scenario. By the way, if you migrate from SAP Process Orchestration to Cloud Integration using the migration tool, you can now select the Pipeline approach, in this case the scenario-specific integration flows and the Partner Directory entries are automatically generated.

Alex

entry.title: How to Schedule "Generate Source List" Fiori App as an Application Job – S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are working in an SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud environment and using the "Generate Source List" Fiori app, which replicates the functionality of transaction ME05 from on-premise systems.

Our requirement is to schedule this app to run periodically using the Application Jobs framework. However, we are facing the following challenges:

  • The "Generate Source List" app does not offer any option to schedule it as an Application Job.

  • We do not see any standard job template for this functionality in the Application Job Catalog.

  • There is no clear documentation or workaround provided for automating this process in Public Cloud.

We are looking for guidance on:

  • Whether SAP provides a released application job template or alternative to schedule source list generation.

  • Any available API, released CDS view, or business service that could be used to implement this process programmatically.

  • Possible workarounds or customer use cases that address this gap.

We appreciate any insights, experiences, or references that can help us automate source list generation in the Public Cloud environment.

Thank you!
Vinay

entry.title: Group Self Intro
entry.content: Hi everyone, I’m Ajay from SAP World working on SAP SuccessFactors. Glad to join the community!

Rgds Trond

entry.title: Re: SAP Community Escape House for June – SAP CAP ❄️ 🏔️
entry.content: @ajay_soreng  thank you for the fun and interesting CAP challenge. Successfully completed.

entry.title: Add SAP PI/PO Communication Channel Start/Stop Functionality to Your iFlows
entry.content:
Greetings SAP Integration Community. My name is Craig Stasila, and I have been in the SAP integration ecosystem for many, many years. It has been a long time since I’ve written a technical blog post, but while I was at SAP Sapphire Orlando recently, a customer that had migrated from SAP PI/PO to SAP Integration Suite posed a question for which I did not have an immediate answer. I promised this person that I would author a blog post with all the details to answer her question, and this is that blog post.

Her question was simple--the answer was not exactly straightforward:

When will SAP Integration Suite have a feature like the SAP PI/PO Communication Channel start/stop control?

On its face, this request sounds like a regular feature request. But the power and flexibility of the Cloud Integration capability of SAP Integration Suite means that you do not have to wait for an official implementation of this feature, and this blog post will tell you how.

Quick sidebar: To create and vote on future feature requests, visit SAP Integration Suite Customer Influence campaign.

The following artifacts have been created to demonstrate the channel start/stop prototype. Each artifact will be explained below:

Figure 1: Package Channel Start/Stop Prototype Artifacts

Mass Controlling Communication Channels and Adapters

The adapter engine component of SAP PI/PO had a feature that allowed for communication channels to be started and stopped through an administration user interface. And, while SAP Integration Suite does not have this exact feature, we can implement a close analog.

We can accomplish this by having our iFlows check the status of a global variable, GlobalChannelStatus for example, and stopping the processing of the iFlow if it has a value of 'STOPPED'.

When to check and what to do if the variable is 'STOPPED' depends on whether the integration is synchronous or asynchronous.

Stopping Channel in Synchronous Integrations

Stopping processing for synchronous interfaces is relatively straightforward. The best practice to stop integration execution for synchronous interfaces is to stop processing and return the call to the sender as soon as possible--before any transformation of the message occurs.

Figure 2: Best-practice to stop synchronous interface processing

When implementing this pattern, handling and reprocessing the messages must be handled by the sender. The logic is implemented in the iFlow _1 Synchronous Check Channel Status.

Figure 3: iFlow _1 Synchronous Check Channel Status

As soon as the synchronous message arrives from the sender, the global variable GlobalChannelStatus is retrieved. A router checks the variable and if the value is 'RUNNING', the message can be transformed as normal and the message can be sent to the receiver system. If, however, the channel status is 'STOPPED', the message is stopped via an exception. I suggest referencing this blog post to create a custom error message so that the sending system receives the correct context that processing has been halted by the administrator.

Stopping Channel in Asynchronous Integrations

Stopping processing for asynchronous interfaces is a bit more involved. In addition to checking the global variable GlobalChannelStatus, we will also be implementing the Retry Pattern with a JMS queue. Unlike the best-practice for synchronous interface processing, for asynchronous processing it is the best practice to hold the message after the message has been transformed--immediately before the message is to be sent to the receiver.

Figure 4: Best-practice to stop asynchronous interface processing

 

Processing for the asynchronous scenario starts like any other interface. I created iFlow A - Build Message Payload to simulate the creation of a message payload whenever the package is deployed. In practice, this will be whatever the standard interface message processing will be. The sender system, channel, and message processing will be unchanged. The only feature that is specific to the channel start/stop processing is the usage of a JMS receiver at the conclusion of the iFlow processing instead of directly calling the receiver system.

Figure 5: iFlow A - Build Message Payload

The iFlow B - Check Channel Status and Send to Adapter is where we will check the status of the global channel variable and implement all of the JMS retry logic. This iFlow will be invoked immediately after  A - Build Message Payload publishes the message to the shared message queue. The iFlow simply retrieves the global variable GlobalChannelStatus. If the value of this variable is 'RUNNING', the message is sent to the receiver system--in this case a iFlow called via the ProcessDirect adapter that simply logs the message payload. If the value of the global variable is 'STOPPED', the processing of the message is stopped and the message will stay in the JMS queue in a Failed status.

Figure 6: iFlow B - Check Channel Status and Send to Adapter

Finally, I created a small iFlow to mock a receiver system. The simple iFlow C - Process Direct Call Adapter logs the message header and body to the message processing log so that it can be viewed in the message monitor.

Figure 7: iFlow C - Process Direct Call Adapter

For this prototype, the global variable GlobalChannelStatus can be set to 'RUNNING' through iFlow D - Start Channels and can be set to 'STOPPED' by deploying iFlow E - Stop Channels. In practice, these iFlows could be triggered externally from an API or through a custom application.

Figure 8: iFlows D - Start Channels & E - Stop Channels

Conclusion

SAP Integration Suite is not a one-for-one reimplementation of SAP PI/PO in the cloud—in many ways it is way more powerful. This blog post is an example of how using SAP Integration Suite as a true platform solution unlocks a whole host of advanced functionality.

Have any of you tried the JMS retry functionality? Can you think of any improvements to what I’ve shown here? I can think of a couple things I could improve but want to hear from you! What would you do to improve this channel start/stop concept?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public, Inventory Valuation 4.5. De...
entry.content:
Hello,

sections 'Determine Range of Coverage' and 'Base Price Lowest of' contain the same Valuation Alternative (VA) in your case (ROC). That means you want to write to the same VA as you read from. That might not be what you want to do.

Leaving the VA in 'Base Price Lowest of' empty means that only the Inventory Price is selected as Base Price. The Base Price is used as the baseline for the devaluation percentage.

From the result list it appears that the system does not have postings in period 1/2025. So, there is nothing to determine, and the 0 results are expected.

You might want to check out the documentation as well, e.g.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/7f47a6d9441c46ac86c96fd27f6015f0/48ae7aab6c9f487e91025791d81db676.html?version=2502.500

I hope this answers your questions.

Regards

entry.title: Work Center Download deletes assigned Resources
entry.content:
We see the issue that the Work Center Download is always deleting the assignes sub-ressources in Digital Manufacturing, Manage Work Centers app. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Work Center Download deletes assigned Resources
entry.content:
The content of the work centers in SAP Digital Manufacturing matches the content of the work centers in SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA. Updates flow from SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA to SAP Digital Manufacturing. If you add anything to the SAP Digital Manufacturing work centers but not to the SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA ones, it gets deleted during the update from SAP ERP or SAP S/4HANA. Note that SAP ERP and SAP S/4HANA are a systems of record for work centers.

This is also mentioned in the Integration Guide https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/integration-guide/work-center-integration?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Business Partner Authorization Tips and Tricks
entry.content:
Hello,
As part of an S/4HANA migration, we replaced all obsolete (VKxx, VDxx, ...) transactions with BP in the SU25 post-processing and maintained them accordingly: The old authorization objects were replaced with the new ones. So far, so good.

However, during testing, we unfortunately discovered that the old objects (V_KNA1*, V_VKO ...) are still being validated 😞

What can we do? Have we missed any steps?

Thanks, Klaus

entry.title: Re: Efficient ABAP Proxy Migration from SAP PO to Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi Jithin,

We also come across the same issue, we have no access to the PO anymore, we can't modify the ESR and re-generate the SPROXY, how can we migrate the inline DTs, can we ignore the warning ones?

BRs,

Rhythm

entry.title: Re: Go-Live Balance Carryforward
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog;

Yes, you’ll still need to run a balance carryforward after your data migration  even if the migration happens after your go-live date.

Here’s why:

Your go-live is set for December 31, 2024, but you’re migrating all transactional data on January 15, 2025.

That means the system won’t automatically know what the opening balances for 2025 should be unless you explicitly tell it and that’s exactly what the balance carryforward does.

It takes your year-end balances from 2024 (like your asset, liability, and equity balances) and brings them into 2025. It also closes out your profit and loss accounts to retained earnings. This step is essential, even if your migration includes detailed journal entries, open items, or other transactional data.

In short:

  • If you’re bringing in just balances, you definitely need to run the balance carryforward.

  • If you’re bringing in full transactional history,  you still need to run it so SAP knows how to start 2025 properly.

So once your data migration is complete (on Jan 15), make sure you run the balance carryforward process.

I hope this will help.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Sap Public Cloud - auto population of the account ass...
entry.content:
Hello,

thanks for your reply. however we are not looking to update the account assignment tab fields but the account assignment category itself at PO line item based on PO type. 

K- Cost center

P-Project

etc.

entry.title: Difference in behavior of action call when called in debug mode vs unattended mode?
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am facing the following issue on one particular action call.

Issue details:

1. Testing the action call directly on the action project works and provides the expected result.

2. Added the same action project as a dependency in an automation. Calling the action call via action groups. When executing this action group call in debug mode, provides the expected result.

3. Only when running the automation in unattended mode, the action call provides an unexpected result.

Already checked the following:

1. oData call on gateway server is working correctly.

2. Latest version is used for both manual and automated testing.

3. Same destination is used in debug and unattended mode.

4. Same data tested in both cases.

I am unsure of the direction I should next take to identify the issue.

Any direction or pointers would be much appreciated.

Best Regards,

Rohit

entry.title: Re: Is there any equivalent app to J3RKKRD / J3RKKRS on S...
entry.content:
Hello @KhongHaGiang 

The business catalogue SAP_FIN_BC_GL_REPORTING_CN_PC is specific to China localisation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. If your system isn't scoped for China, this catalogue won't be available

For broader access to general ledger reporting functionalities, consider assigning the SAP_FIN_BC_GL_REPORT_GLO_PC catalogue.

With kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Sap Public Cloud - auto population of the Confirmatio...
entry.content: hello, thanks for your reply. it's clear for the creation of custom CCK. we are actually wanting to populate automatically in the purchase order the CCK. this was possible on on-premise via the customising point T163LV. Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: Name of iOS app is not showing correctly on iOS and MacOS
entry.content:
Hi, 

After studying the issues you reported, here are some tips how to solve the issues. We are adding these tips also to our documentation. 

 

  1. If you need to have a special characters in the app name e.g. apostrophe, you need to escape the special character with a backslash (\), so for app name Vini d'Italia it would look like this display_name="Vini d\'Italia"
  2. If an app icon (e.g. phone home screen icon) is not updated and you see an error like this in your terminal:
    "No simulator runtime version from ["22C150"] available to use with iphonesimulator SDK version 22E235"
     
    To fix this open Xcode app and go to Settings -> Components and install the latest iOS component (https://github.com/dotnet/macios/wiki/Known-issues-in-.NET8#the-build-fails-with-no-simulator-runtime-version-from-)
    Hopefully these tips are unblocking you.
    BR, Timo

entry.title: Re: Is there any equivalent app to J3RKKRD / J3RKKRS on S...
entry.content:
Thanks. I assigned this business catalog but still can't access these apps: 

- Determine Offsetting Accounts Manually - Russia ( App ID - J3RKKRD )

- Determine Offsetting Accounts - Russia ( App ID - J3RKKRS)

So it means i can only use this app when my system is  scoped for China? 

I understand that the current offsetting account determination logic is applied at the time of posting.

However, in my case, I’ve recently updated the offsetting rule, which means the previous postings no longer align with the new logic. I’d like to know:

- Is there a way to re-trigger the offsetting logic for existing documents based on the updated rule?

- Alternatively, is there any option to manually adjust or split the offsetting accounts for posted items? Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP mock REST service
entry.content: Sorry. You can however provide a custom handler (implementation) for your REST service and manage it by using profiles in package.json for instance. You just need to provide the "impl": "location/to/custom/handler.js"'. In dev you could then have a handler loaded providing a static response if you like. In prod, hybrid, etc you just provide profiles and handlers for these cases.

entry.title: Re: SAP Digital Manufacturing: A Deep Dive into Tool Management Capabilities
entry.content:
Hello @ManoelCosta , @GAkcil 

I was having a look on the possibilities automatic tool reservation that Gizem mention above in this thread.

I have not found the necessary service that could achieve the tool reservation/scheduling, could you please confirm that this is possible in the current release and, if so, point me to the right REST API.

Thanks a lot in advance,

Juan.

entry.title: Re: How to attach the uploaded files of sap BPA to email
entry.content: Hi @ayechan, It would depend on the current payment model that you have with SAP.

entry.title: New printout for purchase order not working
entry.content:
In SSCUI:102272 Fields Relevant to Printouts of Changes， you can define which fields trigger a new printout for purchase orders in case of changes. 

Select the checkbox in column P (Printout) to mark the field as relevant to a printout in case the purchase order is changed.

Define the change text that is to be used in the printout.

However, sometimes it doesn't working well, new output messages are not generated after changing a relevant field on a Purchase order.

To resolve this kind of issue, you need to check all the previously generated output status:If any of the output remains in “In Preparation” or “To be Output”, it does not proceed with new set of output generation(changes Output).As the previous output is not yet print, therefore no new output message will be generated.

To generate a changed output, all previous output should be in “Completed” or “Error” status. Under this scenario, new output message will be generated if any change to the related fields, and the Change Indicator will be marked for the output message which is related to field change.

---------------------------------------------中文----------------------------------------------------------

在 SSCUI:102272 “与变更打印相关的字段” 中，您可以定义当采购订单发生变更时，哪些字段会触发新的打印输出。

 

在 P 列（打印输出）中勾选复选框，可将该字段标记为与采购订单变更时的打印输出相关。
定义要在打印输出中使用的变更文本。

 

然而，有时该功能运行不佳，在采购订单上更改相关字段后，未生成新的输出消息。

 

要解决此类问题，需检查所有先前生成的输出状态：

 

  • 若任何输出状态仍为 “准备中” 或 “待输出”，系统将不会继续生成新的输出集（变更输出）。
  • 由于先前的输出尚未打印，因此不会生成新的输出消息。

 

要生成变更后的输出，所有先前的输出状态应为 “已完成” 或 “错误”。
在此情况下，若相关字段发生任何变更，将生成新的输出消息，且与字段变更相关的输出消息将标记变更指示符。

entry.title: Offsetting valuated absence hrs from IT2001 for a week coinciding with non-worked OT hrs in IT2010
entry.content:
Hello Team;

 

We have a requirement of offsetting and negating any absence hrs. coming from IT2001 corresponding to a week when non-worked OT hrs., WT 8013 in IT2010 received from EC time sheet application. 

 

So our question here is, as our time application is EC time off , can we build this solution within the scope of payroll schema processing in XT00 for IT2010 hrs.' to identify the week of WT coinciding absences in IT2001? Has sap offered any standard operation to achieve it through PCR?

 

Requesting your quick inputs here as we are on production cutover phase for the go live scheduled this month

 

 

 

 

 

Thanks & regards

 

Tandrima

entry.title: Re: Add SAP PI/PO Communication Channel Start/Stop Functionality to Your iFlows
entry.content:
Hi Craig

Cool idea to use the variables for the processing and stop based on it. 

I would also recommend looking at the Pipeline concept

Here you can also undeploy iflows you want to stop but a similary appoach could also be added to the iflows. 

Daniel Graversen /Figaf

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite - Message Metric Question?
entry.content: Do I need a special role to see this information?

entry.title: Where to set the tax-excluded/tax-included accounting method
entry.content:
Hi Dear Experts,

I have two questions regarding SAP's tax-exclusive / tax-inclusive accounting methods:
① Where is the configuration setting located?
② Is the tax-exclusive method the standard (default), and is this correct?

Tax-exclusive accounting method:
Dr. Purchases 90,000 
       Prepaid Consumption Tax 10,000
Cr. Accounts Payable 100,000

Tax-inclusive accounting method:
Dr. Purchases 100,000
Cr. Accounts Payable 100,000

Please suggest.

Thank you very much.Best Regards!

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance

entry.title: Re: Depreciation is calculated on Down payment for asset
entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Re: If This, Then That: Making Sense of Identity Provisioning Conditions
entry.content:
Thank you ivelinakiryakov

 

We are struggling a lot with conditions so great to see you added this to the help section.

Apart from the extra help documentation, has there also been a change in the regex logic of IPS itself. So, are there now new conditions available compared to a month ago?

Back than I was working on a conditional setup and I could not get it to work, thats why I ask.

 

br

Sander

entry.title: How to Back Up CDC
entry.content:
HI SAP,

 

I want to get a backup before working on updating data.
What kind of method is available for GUI operation?
If CUI operation is also possible, please let me know.

 

Best Regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to Back Up CDC
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

Thank you for your inquiry. 

  • Our platform has robust High Availability and Disaster Recovery policies and services in place, these systems are used for internal purposes to recover and/or restore systems and data in case of internal issues.
  • However, we cannot offer the options to use data backups for external use to restore, revert, or roll back changes you have done to your site configuration or data.
  • If sometime in future, you need to have a data recovery solution to restore customer data, you can opt for exporting customer data via dataflow for backup purposes.

You are able to extract user data from CDC Console using Dataflows/Identity Sync solution which is the built in ETL solution of CDC, it is possible to use - REST API accounts.search endpoint in the dataflows this supports retrieval of user data using an SQL-like query syntax, and retrieve data from a large number of user records.  Kinldy check  our external knowledge base for more details: 2699960 - Best Practice: Exporting User Data from Customer Data Cloud

See also:

  • identitySync 
  • accounts.search

Best Regards,

SAP Solution Support

entry.title: Re: Depreciation is calculated on Down payment for asset
entry.content: I'm facing an issue with asset depreciation calculation — depreciation is being calculated on the down payment of the asset PO. How can I prevent depreciation from being calculated on the down payment?

entry.title: Re: Settlement from WBS to cost center of the expenses only
entry.content:
Hi Noy,

it is all written in a message. You need to configure a source structure, where likely you would have Cost and Revenue as two components. Next you can settle only Cost. To avoid settling Revenue have optional settlement in the Settlement profile.

Regards,

Paulo

entry.title: Task list operation: component assignment (OSS note 488765 Do-it-yourself EWB programming refer)
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Has anyone had the opportunity to work with the above OSS note while dealing with the component assignment to a task list operation?
I was trying to follow the note suggestions about placing break-points in the methods (Service functions in CMCL Function Group) but not working.

Any help is highly appreciated, with the goal to have an ABAP code to massively perform the components allocation to operations in task lists.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Previous year's total cannot be displayed in SAC reports
entry.content:
Hi ! 

If you want to display Year-To-Date data for current year and previous year, I suggest you to create a Restricted Measure YTD and after limit date range on your table filters. 

See screens attached, I hope it can help you 

Cynthia
